focus_point: Reading the effective color mode at page appear in ArkTS when EntryAbility sets COLOR_MODE_NOT_SET (follow system): does UIAbilityContext.config.colorMode return the resolved DARK/LIGHT or the raw NOT_SET(-1) at aboutToAppear, and is getUIContext().getHostContext() a valid UIAbilityContext to read it from?
task_excerpt: Initial isDarkMode must reflect the effective system/app color mode at aboutToAppear (deep-color scenario 1). System-follow is set via EntryAbility onCreate setColorMode(COLOR_MODE_NOT_SET). Decision: set isDarkMode = (ctx.config.colorMode === COLOR_MODE_DARK) where ctx = getUIContext().getHostContext() as common.UIAbilityContext.
project_evidence: Index.ets defines COLOR_MODE_DARK=0, COLOR_MODE_LIGHT=1; toggleTheme already does const context = this.getUIContext().getHostContext() as common.UIAbilityContext; context.getApplicationContext().setColorMode(isDarkMode?COLOR_MODE_DARK:COLOR_MODE_LIGHT). EntryAbility.onCreate calls getApplicationContext().setColorMode(ConfigurationConstant.ColorMode.COLOR_MODE_NOT_SET). UI binds via $r() with base/ and dark/ element/color.json palettes.
extra_constraints: Confirm the exact member path config.colorMode and its enum values at runtime when the app follows the system (NOT_SET was set at onCreate).
Confirm getUIContext().getHostContext() returns a UIAbilityContext whose .config.colorMode is readable at aboutToAppear (before any user toggle).
Flag any case where colorMode stays NOT_SET(-1) after setColorMode(NOT_SET) so the equality check isDarkMode = (===DARK) would yield false on a dark system.
Do not redesign; only confirm the exact read path and return/error/null/default semantics.
